text:
ByMichelle Bruton,

Contributor.

Leading up to the Paris 2024 Olympics, American skateboarder Jagger Eaton has a singular focus: to make history by becoming the first skateboarder not only to compete in both disciplines (street and park) at a single Games, but to make the podium in each.

Well,you might be thinking,skateboarding has only been in the Olympics once before, so how historic is this goal?While this is true, what Eaton hopes to accomplish is a rarity for the entire competitive skateboarding landscape, Olympics or otherwise.

Skateboarding is not constructed to enable athletes to compete in multiple disciplines. Many times, events will schedule practice for park during the street final, and vice versa. Eaton’s goal requires hard work and resilience, not to mention help with logistics.

“I do have to do things very differently,”Eaton told mein April. “There’s no comparison to what I do daily, to what skaters doing one event do. To skate both disciplines each day, I have to be more rigorous and disciplined.”

Eaton, 23, made an enormous step toward his ultimate goal this weekend at the first Olympic Qualifier Series (OQS) stop in Shanghai, China. New for the 2024 Games, the OQS will play a large part in determining which athletes in BMX freestyle, breaking, skateboarding and sport climbing qualify for Paris.

The points athletes earn in Shanghai, combined with their world ranking heading into the event and with whatever points they may earn at the second OQS event in Budapest in June, contribute toward Olympic qualification.

For the U.S. Olympic Skateboarding Team, qualification scores will be weighted by 33.3% World Skate Ranking 2022-May 2024, 33.3% OQS Shanghai and 33.3% OQS Budapest.

Coming in to OQS Shanghai, Eaton was ranked No. 2 in the world park rankings and the second American behind Gavin Bottger. In street, he was 12th in the world rankings but the second American behind Nyjah Huston.

In a historic showing in Shanghai, Eaton made the podium in each of the park and street competitions, finishing third in park and winning street.

As evidence of how difficult trying to compete at the top level in two different disciplines is, Eaton was barely able to enjoy his street win before he had to rush out of the arena to prepare for the park final in two hours’ time.

“It’s such a weight lifted off my shoulders, knowing that I can handle this level of contest for five days in a row,” Eaton said,according to the Olympic Information Service (OIS).

“I definitely proved that it can be done, but the question is, can it be repeated? I’m very optimistic. My skating feels unbelievable.”

Eaton is now ranked No. 2 in the world in park and the second American behind Tate Carew, who won the event in Shanghai. In street, Eaton is ranked No. 2 in the world and is the top American, edging Huston by some 2,000 points.

Athletes can earn up to 210,000 points toward park rankings and 260,000 points toward street rankings at each OQS stop.

The Olympic qualification period ends on June 23, and USA Skateboarding will announce the Olympic team shortly thereafter. The combined points at the conclusion of qualifying events will determine each nation’s quotas out of 88 total (44 in each park and street), with one spot in each event reserved for France as the host nation.

Thanks especially to his incredible performance in Shanghai, Eaton is well on his way to making the U.S. team in both disciplines.
title: Jagger Eaton Makes Skateboarding History At Olympic Qualifier Series
author: Michelle Bruton
description: Skateboarder Jagger Eaton won the men's street event and finished third in park at the Olympic Qualifier Series stop in Shanghai on his way to qualifying for Paris 2024.
keywords: Jagger Eaton,Paris 2024 Olympics,OQS Shanghai,Olympic Qualifier Series,us skateboarding team,jagger eaton olympics,nyjah huston,nyjah huston olympics,olympic qualifier series results,olympic qualifier series shanghai
